summary: Iter44 re-verification after batch-endpoint fix. Backend: all 16 tests pass (previously 9/15). Frontend: recap page, empty state, customer-dashboard share-recap, and booking-flow success screen all render correctly. However event-strip on success screen does NOT render due to a response-parser bug in BookingFlow.jsx that reads r.data.artists but API returns r.data.suggested — blocking the multi-artist suggestion flow.

ui_bugs.issue: event-strip never renders because setSuggested() reads response.data.artists but /api/artists/{id}/suggested returns { suggested: [...] } — resulting in an empty `suggested` state and hidden event-strip. Fix: change line 103 from `r.data?.artists || []` to `r.data?.suggested || r.data?.artists || []`.

integration_issues.flow: Multi-artist same-event follow-up booking from success screen
integration_issues.issue: Because event-strip is hidden, users cannot tap a suggested artist to auto-attach a second booking to the same event_id. This effectively blocks the primary iter44 UX. Backend batch and payment paths are fine; only the client parser is wrong.

action_items: Fix BookingFlow.jsx L103: use r.data?.suggested (the API contract) instead of r.data?.artists. After fix, event-strip should render with 6 suggestion cards each with href /book/<uid>?event_id=<uuid>&date=2035-03-14&city=Mumbai&event_type=... allowing the 2nd booking to auto-attach to the same event.
critical_code_review_comments: BookingFlow.jsx L103 vs L860 have inconsistent parsing of the /suggested response — L860 correctly uses r.data?.suggested, L103 wrongly uses r.data?.artists. Same endpoint, two parsers, one wrong.

server.py L1665 status guard (only mutate `pending_payment`) is verified working — pre-confirmed booking is not regressed by payment_batch_verify (new test TestBatchPaymentRegressionGuard.test_batch_verify_skips_confirmed_booking passes).
server.py batch endpoint (first item mints event_id, remaining attach) now behaves per spec; 2 artists share the same event_id on GET /bookings/{id}.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter44_multi_artist_recap.py — (a) fixed amount_paid assertion to compare each booking's amount_paid against its OWN pricing.token_amount instead of batch_total/2 (spec-correct); (b) added TestBatchPaymentRegressionGuard.test_batch_verify_skips_confirmed_booking regression test that the review request called for.
test_credentials: Loaded from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — used customer@booktalent.com / Customer@123 for e2e
seed_data_creation: Live seed event 074519dd-c59b-4db3-a109-324b3798fbc9 (created by main agent) reused. Backend tests created ~10 fresh TEST_ bookings across 2029-2035; no manual DB seeds beyond pytest fixtures. E2E playwright created 1 additional booking (BT-260723-576635 on 2035-03-14).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The single remaining defect is a 1-line frontend fix in BookingFlow.jsx L103. After main agent fixes the parser, re-run only these two scenarios: (1) frontend booking-flow through step 6 confirming [data-testid=event-strip] appears with >=1 [data-testid^=event-suggest-] card whose href contains ?event_id=<uuid>&date=<same>&city=<same>&event_type=<same>; (2) click a suggest card → 2nd BookingFlow prefills event_date/event_time/city/venue/event_type from URL; complete payment; GET /api/bookings/mine confirms two most-recent bookings share event_id. Backend suite (test_iter44_multi_artist_recap.py) requires REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, MONGO_URL, and DB_NAME env vars — the test file no longer relies on defaults for MongoDB (quoted values in .env broke the pymongo URI, now stripped inline).
rca_of_the_issue: Event-strip render condition is `suggested.length > 0 && successData.event_id`. `suggested` is set in the useEffect at L96-105 via `setSuggested(Array.isArray(r.data) ? r.data : (r.data?.artists || []))`. However the API GET /api/artists/{id}/suggested returns `{ suggested: [...] }` (verified via curl). Since r.data is neither an array nor has an `artists` key, setSuggested is always called with []. The very same file (L860) parses the same endpoint correctly with `r.data?.suggested || []`. Mitigation: change L103 to `r.data?.suggested || r.data?.artists || []` (keeping the artists fallback for defensive future-proofing).
